ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Northwest Chinese cold noodles (liangmian) set meal. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Noodle dishes) is the closest defensible parent category. No specific code exists for Northwest Chinese cold noodle set meals.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to Northwest Chinese cold noodles set meal. No suitable parent concept identified for this composite dish.

main_ingredients: sesame paste or chilli oil sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion comprising approximately 300 g cold noodles with sauce and toppings, plus approximately 150 g side dish and approximately 100 g beverage, totalling roughly 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Noodles, Chinese, chow mein, dry (SR Legacy) and composite estimation from USDA Foundation Foods wheat noodle and sesame sauce components
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for Northwest Chinese cold noodles set meal (liangmian tao can). Values estimated by component-based approach: cooked wheat noodles (~55% of dish weight, USDA SR Legacy NDB 20113), sesame-chilli sauce (~15%, USDA Foundation Foods sesame paste and chilli oil), cucumber and vegetables (~20%, USDA), and protein side (~10%, USDA cooked chicken/beef). Per-100g values represent the blended composite. Energy cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA noodle dish analogues. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat noodles and cold noodle analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Northwest Chinese cold noodles set meal. GI of 47 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entry for cold cooked wheat noodles (GI ~47, low category), consistent with retrogradation of starch upon chilling which lowers GI relative to hot noodles. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 129.25) / 100 ≈ 60.7, rounded to 60.6. Serving carbohydrate = 23.5 g/100g × 550 g serving = 129.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce, chilli sauce, and seasoning blend
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sauce preparation, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 420 mg per 100 g (approximately 2310 mg per set meal serving) substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg per meal threshold for sodium-restricted diets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from wheat noodles, partially offset by low GI due to chilling-induced starch retrogradation
type2_diabetes.guidance: The chilled wheat noodles have a lower glycemic index (~47) than hot noodles due to starch retrogradation, but the large serving size delivers a high glycemic load (~60.6 g carbohydrate equivalent), so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion or pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat from sesame paste and chilli oil dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high sodium (~2310 mg per serving) and moderate fat content (predominantly unsaturated from sesame) presents a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request lighter sauce application and avoid adding extra chilli oil to keep saturated fat and sodium within cardioprotective limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Northwest Cold Noodles Set Meal (西北凉面套餐, Xī Běi Liáng Miàn Tào Cān) is a Chinese set meal originating from the northwest regions of China, particularly Shaanxi and Gansu provinces, and served in Chinese noodle restaurants across Singapore. The dish centres on liangmian — cold or room-temperature wheat noodles that are boiled, rinsed under cold water to halt cooking and induce starch retrogradation, then dressed with a bold sauce typically combining sesame paste, chilli oil, black vinegar, soy sauce, minced garlic, and Sichuan peppercorn. Toppings commonly include julienned cucumber, blanched bean sprouts, shredded chicken or beef, and spring onion. The set meal format adds one or two side dishes (such as braised vegetables, a cold appetiser, or a small soup) and sometimes a beverage. The texture is chewy and springy from the wheat noodles, with a complex flavour profile that is simultaneously nutty, spicy, sour, and savoury. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy meal (~924 kcal per set) providing meaningful protein (~34 g), significant carbohydrate (~129 g) from wheat noodles, and notable fat (~32 g) largely from sesame paste and chilli oil. Sodium content is high (~2310 mg per set meal), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The chilling step lowers the glycemic index to approximately 47 (low GI), which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management, though the large carbohydrate load still produces a high glycemic load per serving. The dish is not suitable for gluten-free or sesame-allergic individuals. It is widely available in Lanzhou beef noodle restaurants and northwest Chinese cuisine outlets in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Northwest Chinese Cold Noodles Set Meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component data for cooked wheat noodles, sesame paste, chilli oil, cucumber, and protein components, blended by estimated weight proportions. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database cold wheat noodle analogues. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries; no single approved-source entry for this composite dish was found. 2. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate for a typical set meal (noodles + side + beverage); actual restaurant portions may vary. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this dish type in the component sources used; trace amounts may be present if animal protein is included. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources for this dish. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in plant-oil-based sauces per USDA data. 7. GI of 47 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database cold cooked wheat noodle data; no direct measurement for this specific dish exists. 8. Halal status is listed as false because the restaurant (Lan Ting Xu Lanzhou Beef Noodles) is not confirmed halal-certified; verification required. 9. Glycemic load per serving = (47 × 129.25) / 100 ≈ 60.6, based on 550 g serving at 23.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.
